Interior Plaster in Bastrop County, TX

Star Stucco of Austin brings professional interior plaster work to Bastrop County. Whether you are renovating a historic home in downtown Bastrop, updating a farmhouse near Smithville, or finishing a new build in one of Elgin's growing neighborhoods, we provide hand-applied wall finishes that add lasting quality to your home.

Get a Free EstimateCall (512) 706-9699

Handcrafted Wall Finishes for Bastrop County Properties

Bastrop County combines small-town charm with proximity to Austin, making it an appealing location for homeowners who want more space without leaving Central Texas behind. The historic downtown areas of Bastrop and Smithville contain turn-of-the-century buildings and homes with original plaster walls, while new developments in Elgin and along Highway 71 feature contemporary floor plans that benefit from upgraded wall surfaces.

The Lost Pines region gives Bastrop County a unique landscape that influences local design. Homes built among the pines often incorporate natural materials and earth tones — an aesthetic that pairs naturally with lime wash, warm-toned Venetian plaster, and hand-textured finishes. Interior plaster connects the inside of the home to the organic feel of the surrounding environment.

Our team works in Bastrop County regularly, handling everything from small accent wall projects in weekend retreats to complete interior finishing for new construction. We understand the mix of old and new that defines the area and adjust our approach accordingly.

What's Included With Our Interior Plaster in Bastrop County

Every project includes expert craftsmanship, quality materials, and a commitment to lasting results.

Design Consultation

We visit your Bastrop County home to examine wall conditions, review your design vision, and show you physical samples of the plaster finishes available for your project.

Surface Preparation

Walls are patched, sanded where necessary, and coated with primer. Floors, furnishings, and architectural details are covered and protected before any plaster is applied.

Base Coat Application

A bonding base coat matched to your substrate is applied across all surfaces to be plastered. This layer ensures that the decorative coat adheres properly and cures uniformly.

Decorative Finish Application

Our plasterers hand-apply the finish coat in one or more passes, building up the selected texture with each layer. Every wall is finished with care and consistency.

Color Matching & Tinting

Pigments are blended into the plaster for integral color. We work with Bastrop County homeowners to select tones that complement natural wood, stone, and regional color palettes.

Protective Sealing & Cleanup

A protective topcoat seals the finished plaster, and all coverings and masking are removed. We leave your home in the same condition we found it — except for the beautiful new walls.

Common Interior Plaster Needs in Bastrop County

Bastrop County homeowners turn to interior plaster for a variety of projects. These are the requests we encounter most often.

Smooth Wall Finishes

Standard spray textures feel out of place in many Bastrop County homes, especially those with exposed wood beams or reclaimed materials. A smooth skim coat creates a refined backdrop that lets those elements stand out.

Decorative Plaster Accents

Feature walls in living rooms, around fireplaces, and in entryways are ideal spots for decorative plaster. These projects add a layer of craftsmanship without requiring a full-home commitment.

Venetian Plaster

The deep, polished look of Venetian plaster works well in the custom homes and renovated properties found throughout Bastrop County, lending formal rooms a timeless quality.

Interior Wall Restoration

Bastrop and Smithville's historic homes often have aging plaster walls with cracks, loose sections, or water damage. We restore these surfaces carefully, preserving the building's original character.

Custom Textures for Feature Walls

From rustic adobe-inspired surfaces to refined linen textures, custom feature walls give Bastrop County homeowners a way to express personal style through their walls.

Skim Coating Over Textured Walls

A skim coat over existing texture is a popular update for Bastrop County homes being prepared for resale or simply refreshed for a cleaner, more modern look.

Why Choose Us for Interior Plaster in Bastrop County?

  • Hands-on artisan skill from plasterers who understand traditional and modern application techniques
  • Comprehensive finish options from rustic hand-troweled textures to polished decorative surfaces
  • Color expertise that allows us to create warm, natural tones suited to Bastrop County aesthetics
  • Thorough interior protection that safeguards flooring, trim, and finishes throughout the project
  • Painstaking detail work that produces clean lines, seamless transitions, and professional results
  • Flexible solutions designed for the mix of historic renovations and new builds found in the area

Licensed & Insured

Full liability coverage and workers' compensation for every project in Bastrop County.

20+ Years Experience

Decades of hands-on stucco work across Central Texas residential and commercial properties.

Certified Applicators

Certified by Sto Corp, Senergy/BASF, Master Wall, Dryvit, and Parex LaHabra for the highest quality results.

Our Interior Plaster Process

A straightforward approach from start to finish — no surprises, no shortcuts.

1

Design Consultation

We visit your Bastrop County property to assess walls and discuss options. You see and touch real finish samples before making any commitment.

2

Surface Preparation & Priming

All surfaces are prepped and primed. We give special attention to older walls in historic Bastrop homes, addressing underlying issues before plaster goes on.

3

Plaster Application & Finishing

Base and finish coats are applied in sequence. Our plasterers maintain consistent coverage and texture across every wall, adjusting technique as needed for each room.

4

Final Review & Touch-Ups

We walk through the project with you, examining walls under various lighting. Any spots needing attention are refined, and the sealer is applied to complete the job.
